nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=170 beats='Wren Callahan steps off the last bus into Saltmere, the harbor town where she was born, and finds it half a stranger to itself; the fishermen's memorial lists names no one in the quay can place, her cousin Milo waves at a house Wren remembers as a chandlery, and when she says the word chandlery three people give her the same polite blank look; only Wren seems to carry the missing years, the vanished shopfronts, the empty chairs still pulled up to kitchen tables, and four threads tug at her at once, the harbor office records, the last living harbor-keeper on Gull Point, the family photographs that will not stay the same, and the sealed undercroft beneath the old net house'>>

choices.label: Search the harbor office for the missing years.
Climb Gull Point to the last harbor-keeper.
Lay out the family photographs that keep changing.
Force the sealed undercroft under the net house.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='The harbor office smells of dust and floor wax; the harbor roll of Saltmere births and deaths lies open, but whole pages are blank where names were inked, the paper faintly warm as if the words left only moments ago, and the young harbor clerk keeps losing his place mid-sentence'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren leans across the counter and asks the harbor clerk who lived at number nine on the quay, and watches him grope for a memory that keeps sliding out of reach'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The harbor clerk whispers that he keeps a photograph of a girl he is sure is his daughter, though he cannot summon her name or her face the moment he looks away from it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren copies the blank entries into her own notebook, off the harbor roll, hoping ink she carries out of Saltmere will hold where the harbor roll ink will not'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The harbor clerk hands her a magnifier and together they find a birth entry where the name has thinned to a grey smear, present and absent at once'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren asks the harbor clerk to say aloud a name he cannot hold, and his hands shake so hard the pen rolls off the desk, and she must choose between mercy and the answer she needs'>>

choices.label: Steady him and read on together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='The harbor clerk panics, shoves Wren out and bolts the hall, and the one door into the Saltmeres records closes against her'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The lease manifest runs back two centuries in a dozen different hands, cracked and foxed, smelling of the same warm dust as the blank harbor roll'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren traces the fortieth-year clause across the centuries and finds it always followed a hard season, a failed harvest, a fever, a flood, some grief the Saltmere could not carry'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='In the margin beside the newest clause someone has drawn a small tree with keel-roots far deeper than its branches, and inked beneath it the single word enough'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Every fortieth year the same clause reappears in careful script, a promise that the harbor shall keep a little in exchange for a fruitful season'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The repeating clause always names a keeper, and Wren can read the line calmly forward or tear the whole page free in fright'>>

choices.label: Trace the clause forward in the manifest.
Rip the founding lease free and run.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Outside, the ink fades from the stolen page to nothing in Wren fist, her one hard fact turned to blank paper'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The last named keeper, inked forty years ago, was Wren’s own grandmother, and calmer now the harbor clerk unlocks a back room where the forfeited years are shelved as sealed envelopes, one of them warm to the touch'>>

choices.label: Follow it down toward the keel-root.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren breaks the warm seal and finds a photograph of the net house undercroft door standing open on darkness'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='A second envelope holds a crew photograph with a whole row of children scratched out, and a note reading they were the price of the year the mill burned'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren tucks the warm photographs inside her coat against her skin, as if her own heat could keep the images from fading like everything else in this Saltmere'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Pencilled on the back of the first photograph in a careful hand are the words it drinks from the keel-root, come before the next neap tide'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='The envelope, the harbor roll, the lease all point the same way, down to the net house undercroft where something keeps the years'>>

choices.label: Carry what you found to the net house.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='Gull Point is all wind and bent apple trees, and at the top Thess Marlow, the last harbor-keeper, sits pruning by feel because her eyes have gone; she knows Wren by the smell of the road on her, and says you are the one it cannot swallow, sit'>>

choices.label: Ask Thess what is eating Saltmere.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Thess speaks of the Hush, the slow forgetting that rises from the drowned mast when the Saltmere forgets to feed it a name'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She tells how the founders knelt at the tide pool in a starving winter and begged the harbor to take their grief, and how it learned that grief is only memory with the edges still sharp'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Every keeper since has fed it one willing name a year, and the last to do so before the line broke was Wren’s own grandmother'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She asks whether Wren is brave or only stubborn, because the two look alike right up until the very end'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Thess waits for Wren to say what she means to do about the hunger on the hill, whether to starve it blindly or learn its price first'>>

choices.label: Ask what feeding it a name would truly cost.
Swear to starve it and feed it nothing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Thess decides Wren is a fool who will only make it hungrier, and sends her down the hill with a warning she cannot understand'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The keeper keeps a knotted net, one knot for every keeper back to the founders, hung from a nail worn smooth by generations'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Thess guides Wren thumb over each knot and names the keeper it stands for, her voice steady until it reaches the knots no living mind can attach to a face'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Past those the net is smooth for a whole arm length, one lost keeper after another, a rope of forgotten guardians holding a Saltmere that does not know it is held'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The knots past a certain year are worn slick, as if handled by hands that no longer remember tying them'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The net runs long in Wren’s hands, and she can trace the knots back to the founding with Thess, or cut the line to try to end it'>>

choices.label: Let Thess guide your fingers along the knots.
Cut the net to break the line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren saws the keeper net in two and the loose knots scatter down the slope, cutting the last thread anyone held to the vanished years'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The net ends at her grandmother knot, still tight and warm, with one fresh empty knot tied past it waiting for a name, and Thess presses a withered shell into Wren palm, the mark every keeper carries'>>

choices.label: Follow it down toward the keel-root.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Thess says this is why the Hush slides off you, you were never planted here, and describes the black tide pool under the net house where the founding keel-root learned to drink'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The shell will green only in a hand the Hush cannot touch, she says, and a green shell is the founders token, the sign that its bearer may bargain at the tide pool'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She cannot come down the hill on her own failing legs, but she promises to send Milo to the net house before the neap tide so Wren will not face the water alone'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She warns that the keel-root keeps what it takes and the first neap tide is only days away now'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='Thess last words send Wren to the undercroft, where the oldest keel-root has cracked the founding wall and waits below in the dark'>>

choices.label: Carry what you found to the net house.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='In Milo loft the family photographs will not hold still; a christening portrait loses a godmother between two blinks, a harvest crowd thins by a face each time Wren looks away, and the empty spaces heal over as if the missing were never there'>>

choices.label: Try to catch the change in the act.
Search for a photograph that will not change.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren lays every photograph on the floorboards and pins the corners flat, determined to catch the erasure as it happens'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She films the pinned photographs on her phone, and on the small bright screen the vanishing happens slower, a face dimming frame by frame like a coal going grey'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='When she plays the film back the erased faces are gone from the recording too, as if the Hush reaches even into the memory of a machine that only borrowed the image'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='For one heartbeat a woman stands at the harbor gate, and then she is only a gap in the grass'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren can photograph the photographs before they change, or panic and burn the fading ones to try to stop the bleeding'>>

choices.label: Photograph them and study who vanishes first.
Burn the fading prints in a panic.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren sets a match to the fading prints, and the fire takes the last images of the forgotten, so not even the gaps remain to prove anyone was lost'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='One logbook feels wrong in the hand, heavier, its images sharp and whole while everything around them fades'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The strange logbook has no people in it at all, only the harbor through the seasons, the drowned mast standing a little taller and a little darker in every passing year'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Pressed between two pages Wren finds a coil of rope and a faded ribbon tied with thread, saved by someone who knew the pictures of people would not last'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Every picture in it is of the harbor, the net house, the drowned mast, as if the Hush guards its own face'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren can compare the untouched harbor photographs across the years, or close the strange logbook and walk away from it'>>

choices.label: Compare the harbor photographs year by year.
Seal the logbook and walk away.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren closes the strange logbook, telling herself photographs are only paper, and by morning cannot recall why the loft mattered, the thread slipping even from her'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Laid in order the harbor photographs show the drowned mast swelling year by year while the Saltmere around it grows thin, and behind the last photograph, tucked in the frame, is Wren’s grandmother’s young face beside the undercroft door holding the same withered shell'>>

choices.label: Follow it down toward the keel-root.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='On the logbook final page, in her grandmother hand, are the words go down to the keel-root before neap tide, it keeps the years there'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren compares the christening photograph to the logbook and realises the missing godmother was a keeper, remembered by the harbor while the Saltmere forgot her face'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She understands now why the harbor photographs never change, the keel-root protects the image of the thing that feeds it, guarding its own long hunger against forgetting'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The photographs, whole and fading alike, all point Wren at the net house undercroft'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='The changing pictures and the whole ones agree, the answer waits below the net house where the keel-root drinks'>>

choices.label: Carry what you found to the net house.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='The net house leans empty at the harbor edge, and its undercroft door is sealed with a bar of harbor wood grown over by living bark, as if the tree itself reached up to hold the door shut against anyone who would open it'>>

choices.label: Test how deep the cold air goes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren works the ropelocked bar loose splinter by splinter, and cold air breathes up from below'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The living bark bleeds a thin cider-smelling sap where her tools bite, and the whole door seems to flinch, wood shrinking from her like a hand pulled back from a flame'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Through the widening gap she can see the first steps slick with a neap tide that has no business being there in the mild autumn air of the harbor above'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The draught carries a smell of apples and old water and something older underneath them both'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The open door gapes on darkness, and Wren can descend toward the pulsing light or lose her nerve and reseal it'>>

choices.label: Descend toward the pulsing light below.
Reseal the door and pretend you never came.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren hammers the bark bar back into place and walks away, telling herself some doors are shut for good reason, and the Saltmere keeps losing its years'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren drops a pebble down the dark stair and counts, and the count runs longer than the little building could ever hold'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The pebble never lands, or lands so far down that its knock returns as a whisper, and the cold that rises with the echo carries the smell of a hundred old kitchens'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She lowers her lamp on its cord and the flame leans sideways, drawn downward as if the dark itself were breathing in, patient and slow and endlessly hungry'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The steps descend past shelves of cider gone to vinegar toward a faint light that is not lamplight, pulsing slow like breath'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren can follow the light down slowly, marking her steps, or retreat while she still remembers why she came'>>

choices.label: Mark the steps and go down slowly.
Retreat up the stair while you still can.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Halfway down Wren feels her own reason for coming thin and drain away, and she climbs back into daylight emptyhanded, the undercroft having drunk her very purpose'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='At the bottom the split wall lets the drowned mast taproot through, down into a black tide pool, and the water is not still, it holds faces and streets and seasons turning slowly in the deep'>>

choices.label: Follow it down toward the keel-root.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The tide pool shows Wren her own reflection last of all, and hers alone does not dissolve into the drift of the drowned Saltmere'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren kneels at the lip of the black water and the drowned faces turn up toward her lamp, mouths moving, asking to be remembered by anyone at all who still can'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Her grandmother’s face drifts among them, younger than Wren ever knew her, and for one moment it steadies and seems to smile before the current turns it under again'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Scratched into the wet stone above the water are the words the keel-root keeps, the neap tide sets it, remember for us all'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='The undercroft, the tide pool, the drowned faces, every thread has led Wren to this same black water beneath the tree'>>

choices.label: Carry what you found to the net house.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=175 beats='However Wren came, every thread ends at the split founding wall beneath the net house, at the black tide pool where the drowned mast taproot drinks; here the Hush has a shape at last, not a curse but a bargain, the founders traded the Saltmeres memory for a harvest that never fails, letting the keel-root keep a little each season, and the keeping has grown greedy, swallowing years and faces and whole lives while the Saltmere smiles and forgets it ever had them; the tide pool turns with everything Saltmere has lost, and Wren must decide how to face a thing that eats remembering itself'>>

choices.label: Reach into the tide pool to understand it.
Refuse it and try to wall the keel-root off.
Run and call Saltmere to witness this.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren cups the black water and it does not take her memory, it shows her more, the founders kneeling at this tide pool long ago'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The tide pool is warm as blood against her fingers and it does not pull at her, it only offers, showing the founders faces smoothing as their grief is drawn away down into the keel-root'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She sees a keeper walk willingly into the keel-roots embrace so the Saltmere could forget a grief too heavy to carry through a hard winter'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The water offers Wren a deeper truth if she will hold it a moment longer, or she can draw her hand back cold and unchanged'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren holds the water too long and it shows her only the drowned faces turning, until she pulls back shaking, no wiser and much colder'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The tide pool teaches her that the keel-root cannot hold what it never touched, and Wren grew up far from Saltmere, her memories banked elsewhere'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The lesson settles in her like cold water finding its level, that the girl the Saltmere cannot swallow might be the one who can make it give back'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='That distance is why the Hush slides off her, and it may be the one thing that lets her reach in and take memory back out'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren tries to pack the split wall with stone and harbor pitch, but the keel-root only drinks around her hands, patient and unbothered'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The pitch she packs turns to slurry and runs between her fingers into the tide pool, and the keel-root drinks even that, taking the very stone she meant to stop it with'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She understands you cannot wall off a hunger, you can only feed it or fill it with something it will keep'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='The greening shell in Wren palm steadies the faces where she touches the water, and she can learn from that or keep uselessly mortaring the wall'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren mortars the wall shut and for one proud hour it holds, then the keel-root splits it wider than before and a whole childhood of the Saltmere drains away in the night'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Where Wren untaken memory touches the tide pool the drowned faces steady, as if her outsideness were a stone the current cannot move'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She sees she has been trying to plug a mouth when she should have been meeting a need, and a need can be answered with a gift as easily as with a theft'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Stopping the Hush is not about closing a door but about giving the keel-root something it can keep forever instead of the living'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren runs up into the quay shouting the names from the blank harbor roll, and a few townsfolk flinch as if the sounds fit old wounds'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She grabs old Old Cobb by the sleeve and says your wife, Mara, the bait stall, and for a heartbeat his eyes fill and then go smooth again, the grief wiped clean before it can land'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Most only stare, unable to hold a grief for people they can no longer recall, their faces closing like shutters'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Milo keeps his memory a moment longer than the rest, and Wren can build on his stubborn love or shout herself hoarse alone'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='The quay empties around Wren as she shouts, and by dusk even Milo looks at her strangely, and she stands alone with a fist of names no one will take'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Milo tells her the Hush is weakest where love is stubborn, where someone flatly refuses to let go of a face'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='One held memory is a crack of light under a shut door, proof that the Hush can be resisted if enough hands refuse to let go all at once'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='If enough of the Saltmere could be made to remember at once, on purpose, their held memory might anchor what the keel-root keeps stealing'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=175 beats='The threads knot into one truth: Wren is immune because she grew up beyond Saltmere, her memories rooted in soil the Hush never owned, and that same outsideness is a key; she alone can reach into the tide pool and lift memory back out without being drunk down with it; Thess withered shell, greening now in her palm, is the founders old token, the mark a keeper carries, and it will let the keel-root recognise her as one who may bargain; but a bargain has a price, and the keel-root does not give years back for nothing, so Wren must learn what stopping the Hush will cost before she can choose how much to pay'>>

choices.label: Weigh giving the keel-root a memory of your own.
Weigh taking the empty keepers place yourself.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren offers the tide pool a small memory to test its hunger, the taste of her grandmother kitchen on a Sunday'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The returned wharf smells of coal smoke and Sunday baking, solid enough to walk down, and then the keel-root gently reminds her it is only lent, and the price is not yet paid'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The keel-root takes it gently and gives back, for one breath, a whole vanished wharf of houses shimmering into being'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='So it is a trade, memory for memory, the beloved for the lost; Wren can measure the true cost or snatch her hand back now.'>>

choices.label: Snatch your hand back and keep everything.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Frightened by how much the keel-root will take, Wren pulls free and keeps every memory clenched tight, and the Saltmere goes on quietly losing itself around her'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=145 beats='The keel-root will hold whatever she gives it forever, meaning whatever she surrenders she can never have again; she can follow the cost fully or give something rashly.'>>

choices.label: Follow the cost to its end.
Let it take a memory you cannot spare.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren lets the tide pool take her grandmother whole, and the wharf returns, but she stands in it a stranger, unable to recall why the houses should mean anything at all'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren understands the true price now, to restore the Saltmere she must feed the keel-root something she loves enough to lose'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The arithmetic is plain and cruel, the Saltmere can be bought back memory for memory, and the buyer must be someone the Hush cannot bill for its own'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The more she is willing to give, the more of Saltmere will come rising back out of the deep'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren feels the fresh unnamed knot on Thess net pull at her wrist, the empty keeper place waiting to be filled'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The empty knot warms against her wrist like a living thing, eager, and she feels how easy it would be to simply give herself and let the whole Saltmere forget its grief in peace'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She understands she could tie herself to it, become the one the harbor remembers so the Saltmere need not be devoured'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='A keeper is remembered only by the keel-root and forgotten by everyone else; Wren can study that fate or refuse it outright.'>>

choices.label: Decide that no one should have to vanish.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren refuses the keeper knot and walks away from the net, and without a keeper the keel-root simply keeps eating, until by winter Thess herself is a name Wren alone remembers'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=145 beats='The price is to be slowly erased from the living world even while holding it together from below; she can grasp the whole of it or tie the knot too soon in fear.'>>

choices.label: Follow the cost to its end.
Tie yourself to the keeper knot right now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren ties the fresh knot around her own wrist before she understands the whole of it, and the Saltmere steadies while she begins to fade from Milo’s eyes, a keeper made in fear'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren sees the shape of the sacrifice, a keeper anchors the Saltmere by letting the harbor keep her instead of the many'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='A keeper is loved only by the harbor and lost to everyone above, and Wren is not ready to become a name that only a tree remembers, not yet, not in fear'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='It is a life spent as the thing that remembers so that others may forget their grief in peace'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=180 beats='Now Wren knows the price in full; the keel-root will give the Saltmere back its stolen years, but only if it is given something to keep forever in their place, either a memory beloved enough to matter or a keeper willing to be remembered by the harbor and forgotten by the world; Thess climbs down the undercroft stair to stand with her, and Milo comes too, refusing to let Wren face the tide pool alone, and their being there changes the arithmetic, because a price paid by one is a wound and a price shared by many is only a scar; at the black tide pool beneath the drowned mast, with the neap tide gathering above, Wren must choose not whether to pay but how, and who pays alongside her'>>

choices.label: Ask the whole Saltmere to share the weight.
Offer the keel-root one thing to save one soul.
Bargain only to understand, not to win.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren sends Milo running through Saltmere with the harbor roll names, gathering everyone who will come to the net house before neap tide'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Milo comes back breathless with a dozen townsfolk behind him, then a dozen more, drawn by a feeling they cannot name that tonight they must not forget on purpose'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The plan is to make the Saltmere remember together, out loud, all at once, so no single heart is drained to fill the keel-root'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren can teach the keel-root a new bargain of shared keeping, or let the Saltmere simply make peace with its smaller losses.'>>

choices.label: Let Saltmere forgive the harbor and go home.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=135 beats='Wren tells the gathered Saltmere the harbor was only lonely, and they choose to live with the losses, keeping what they have and mourning nothing they cannot name, a quiet peace with a quiet grief'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=145 beats='The weight must land on enough hands at once or it will crush the few who came; she can wait for all or rush the ritual.'>>

choices.label: Begin before all Saltmere has arrived.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren begins before half the Saltmere arrives, and the weight lands too heavy on too few, and the ones who came stagger back forgetting their own names, the chain snapping for want of hands'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Thess shows Wren how to teach the keel-root a keeping spread across a hundred hearts rather than devouring one'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Thess’s voice reaches them from the top of the stair, teaching the old words that turn a devouring into a keeping, a hunger fed by one into a memory held by all'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The living will hold the Saltmere memory between them like a chain, and the keel-root will keep through many instead of hollowing anyone'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren cannot empty the whole tide pool, so she chooses one, kneeling with a single face fixed in her mind'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She fixes the harbor girl in her mind, the gap-toothed grin, the way she always won at skipping stones, a whole small life the Saltmere lost the same winter Wren was taken away'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='It is that girl she means to lift back into the light no matter the cost'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren can brace to trade a piece of her own heart for the one soul, or simply learn who she would have saved.'>>

choices.label: Only ask the keel-root to show you her face.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=135 beats='Wren asks the tide pool only to show her who she would save, and it lifts a face to the surface, and she carries the knowing back up unspent, changed by it, having taken nothing and given nothing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=145 beats='The keel-root offers one memory of Wren’s own for one soul restored; she can pay the fair price or try to cheat it.'>>

choices.label: Demand the keel-root give without taking anything.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren tries to pull one soul free while surrendering nothing, and the keel-root closes hard, keeping both the lost one and the hour she wasted, and she surfaces having saved no one'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren braces to give up one precious thing so that one lost person may walk Saltmere again, remembered by all'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='One rescue, complete and clean, is a candle lit against a dark she cannot flood with light, and she decides a single saved life is not too small a thing to matter'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='It is a small and complete mercy in a sea she knows she cannot empty by herself'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Wren decides she does not need to defeat the Hush, only to understand it well enough to live honestly beside it'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The keel-root shows her the founders not as villains but as parents who could not bury one more child and still rise to prune the trees, and who made a terrible mercy of forgetting'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She asks the keel-root not for years but for the whole truth of the bargain the founders made in their grief'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=150 beats='Wren can accept an even trade for a single year, or try to force the whole hard truth into the Saltmere at once.'>>

choices.label: Take back one year on the keel-roots own terms.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=135 beats='Wren accepts the old bargain and asks for a single year back, the year she was born here, and the keel-root gives it and keeps a year of hers, a fair and bittersweet swap that leaves her both fuller and lighter'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=145 beats='The keel-roots long memory is a grief no one mind can hold alone; she can carry it wisely or pour it into the Saltmere uninvited.'>>

choices.label: Force the whole truth into every mind at once.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren tries to pour the keel-roots long memory into every mind in Saltmere, and the Saltmere reels, unable to bear a grief handed to them whole, and they turn from her, choosing forgetting over a truth too heavy'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='The tide pool opens its long memory to Wren, the hard winters, the mercy and the theft tangled together as one old unhealed wound'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='She means to meet the drowned mast the way you meet an old grief in the family, not to defeat it but to finally say aloud the names it has carried alone for two hundred years'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Carrying that understanding, she means to face the drowned mast not as an enemy but as the keeper of a sorrow the whole Saltmere must finally own'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=180 beats='Every path brings Wren to the same place at last, the black tide pool beneath the drowned mast with the first neap tide silvering the harbor overhead, the Saltmere gathered on the stair or a single face held in her mind; the withered shell in her palm is green and whole now, the keeper token alive again, and the keel-root waits, ancient and patient, ready to make its bargain; this is the hinge of everything, the moment the Hush can be turned from a hunger into a keeping, from a theft into a shared remembering, or lost for another forty years; whatever Wren carries down to the water now, the Saltmere memory, a single name, an honest grief, she must offer it here and see what the harbor chooses to give back'>>

choices.label: Anchor the whole Saltmeres memory at once.
Bind the shared vow of the living into the keel-root.
Save the one soul you came to lift.
Accept the bittersweet trade and let go.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=155 beats='Wren presses the greening shell to the black water and speaks the founders bargain backward, not the harbor keeps for us but we keep for the harbor, and the keel-root shudders as a hundred living memories pour down the undercroft stair at once'>>

choices.label: Steer the flood of memory together.
Falter as the current grows too strong.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='The flood of remembering is more than Wren can steer alone, and it scatters before it can anchor, leaving the Saltmere dazed and the keel-root fed just enough to sleep, the great chance spent for a fragile partial calm'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=160 beats='With Thess’s hands over hers and Milo’s voice leading the crowd, Wren teaches the keel-root the new bargain, and the tide pool stops turning like a drain and begins to turn like a wheel, holding the Saltmere’s memory for it rather than swallowing it'>>

choices.label: Seal the new keeping into the keel-root.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=155 beats='Wren asks the gathered Saltmere not to give up anything they love but to promise together, aloud, to remember, and the vow rises off them like warmth off a fire, and the keel-root leans toward it, curious at a thing offered freely'>>

choices.label: Bind the vow into the keel-root.
Lose the crowds nerve at the last moment.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='The crowds nerve fails as the tide pool stirs, and one by one they climb back toward the light, and Wren is left holding a vow no one will finish speaking, the keel-root settling back to its slow patient theft'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=160 beats='The shared vow spreads thin across every heart in the undercroft so no single person is drained, and the keel-root, offered a keeping it can hold without hollowing anyone, accepts the new bargain the way dry ground accepts rain'>>

choices.label: Seal the shared keeping for good.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=170 beats='The keel-root accepts the shared keeping and the split wall knits shut with living bark, and up in the harbor the vanished years come rising back into the Saltmere like dawn filling a valley, streets, faces, the chandlery, the harbor girl, all of it remembered at once by everyone who chose to hold it'>>

choices.label: Watch Saltmere wake into its whole self.
Notice the one gap the flood did not reach.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=155 beats='Saltmere wakes whole, every stolen year restored and now held safe by a Saltmere that remembers on purpose, the harbor bearing fruit without eating its keepers, and Wren stands in a quay full of names that finally fit their faces, the girl who taught a Saltmere to keep itself'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='One face stays missing, Wren’s own grandmother, the last keeper, whose memory the keel-root has held longest and deepest, and Wren realises she can leave her in peace or reach once more for the one soul the flood passed by'>>

choices.label: Let her rest and keep the rest whole.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=150 beats='Wren reaches one last time and lifts her grandmother up out of the deep water, and the old keeper opens her eyes in a Saltmere that suddenly remembers loving her, and the two of them stand in the restored harbor, the long debt paid and the family whole again'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=150 beats='Wren lets her grandmother rest where she chose to be, remembered now by the keel-root as the keeper who gave the Saltmere its second chance, and Saltmere lives on whole around that one gentle absence, a scar it will honour instead of forget'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=155 beats='Wren holds one face steady in her mind, the harbor girl lost the winter she left, and offers the keel-root a single memory of her own in fair trade for that one life lifted back into the Saltmere'>>

choices.label: Give the memory freely for the one.
Hesitate over which memory to lose.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren hesitates too long over which piece of herself to give, and the neap tide closes over the tide pool for another year, and the harbor girl stays a face in a fading photograph, so close to saved that the near miss aches'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=160 beats='Wren gives up her own first summer in Saltmere, a small bright thing she can spare, and the keel-root takes it gently and lifts the harbor girl up out of the water, blinking, alive, remembered again by a Saltmere that mourned her without knowing why'>>

choices.label: Bring the one you saved up into the light.
Ask if you might save one more.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=155 beats='Wren climbs the undercroft stair with the harbor girl beside her into a Saltmere that gasps to remember her, and though the Hush still sleeps below and the wider loss remains, one whole life walks Saltmere again, bought back with a summer Wren will never miss as much as this is worth'>>

<<FILL role=success words=150 beats='The keel-root will give no more than the fair one for one, and Wren accepts it, choosing not to bleed herself dry chasing everyone, and she walks home with the harbor girl and a hard gentle wisdom, that saving one completely is worth more than failing at saving all'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=155 beats='Wren kneels at the water not to win but to be honest with it, and she offers the keel-root her own memory of the day she first understood the Saltmere was disappearing, trading her certainty for a single year given back to Saltmere'>>

choices.label: Accept the even trade and let go.
Grasp for more than the keel-root will give.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren asks for more years than she offered, and the keel-root, patient and exact, keeps what she gave and grants nothing, and she surfaces poorer by one memory and richer by no restored year, taught the cost of grasping'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=160 beats='The keel-root takes Wren certainty and gives back the year she asked for, the chandlery on the quay with its warm lit window, and she understands she has not defeated the Hush but made an honest peace with it, a fair exchange between a girl and an old grief'>>

choices.label: Carry the one restored year home.
Promise the keel-root a visit every neap tide.
Hold back one thing you will never give.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=145 beats='Wren climbs into a Saltmere with one more street than it had that morning, carrying a little less of herself and a little more of Saltmere, and settles into the bittersweet knowledge that some things return only when we agree to let other things go'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=155 beats='Wren promises the keel-root a yearly visit, a keeper who tends rather than surrenders, trading a single memory each neap tide for a single year returned, and she chooses to spend her life this way, slowly buying Saltmere back one season at a time, neither wholly winning nor losing'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=125 beats='Unwilling to feed the keel-root anything she truly loves, Wren keeps back one thing it can never take, a single photograph of the harbor gate held so tight it creases, and she walks home with the Saltmere still thinning around her, one saved image of everything lost'>>
